MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KEVIN FELTS CO, 'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND DISMISS THE COMPLAINT FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. This case was transferred to Lake County on January 3, 2014. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2013 Continued To Feb-26-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Transfer Of Venue
Off Calendar Dec-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GERLAD CAREY STERNS, TEL. NO. (510) 267-0500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JONATHAN S. MORSE OF THE MORSE LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. (805) 719-2695 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT TOM WASSON'S Motion For Transfer Of Venue to Lake County is granted, and Plaintiffs' countermotion to retain jurisdiction in San Francisco is denied. This is a personal injury action, and none of the defendants reside or have their principal place of business in San Francisco. (CCP 395(a).) The moving defendants reside and have their principal place of business in Lake County, and the other defendants are out-of-state corporations. Venue is proper in Lake County, and the motion to transfer venue was timely made. (CCP 396b.) Defendants filed their motion to transfer at the time they answered the Complaint. As to Plaintiffs' countermotion, Plaintiffs have not demonstrated that this Court should retain jurisdiction based on the convenience of witnesses. (See Witkin, California Procedure, Action 946, p. 1168.) In their declarations, Plaintiffs' witnesses Bruce Borden, Arin Kumar and Abdon Llorente do not state the nature of the testimony expected. Further and significantly, they have not shown that the inconvenience of traveling to Lake County warrants retaining venue in San Francisco; each witness intends to bill for his travel time. Further, Plaintiff Ronald Broyle has not sufficiently demonstrated that his health would be endangered by traveling to Lake County. Mr. Broyle has not proffered any current medical reports; the last medical report is dated September 6, 2012. No medical professional has opined that Mr. Broyle is too ill or feeble to travel to Lake County without endangering his health. The Plaintiff has not shown how the ends of justice are served by retaining jurisdiction in San Francisco. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Notice Of Motion For Transfer Of Venue
Off Calendar Nov-18-2013 Continued To Dec-09-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TOM WASSON'S Motion For Transfer Of Venue IS Continued to December 9, 2013 to permit Plaintiffs the opportunity to file a supplemental brief in response to the evidence proffered in reply. Any supplemental brief shall be filed and served by December 2, 2013. Courtesy copies are to be provided to Dept. 302 on the date of filing. (SFLR 2.6B.) Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
